source: Generated from optimized diagnostic exam JSON files. Older files were used only for validation/reference.
intended_use: General answer-key/calibration seed keyed by question_key and answer_value. It is richer than the current calibration_weights table.
db_note: Existing public.calibration_weights can only store question_id, answer_value, weight_da, weight_de, weight_ds, aptitude_category. Use the legacy file for that table, or create a richer answer-key table/use this as a repo JSON read by Railway.

normalization_maps.track_to_weights: O*NET only: DE/DS/DA mapped traits get selected Likert value as direct calibration weight; null track_mapping gives zero direct track weights.

rows.selected_label: Talk to them privately, understand the issue, and help plan a solution.
Finish their work yourself to avoid any further delays.
Ignore the issue and focus only on your tasks.
Support the decision professionally despite your disagreement.
Calmly raise your concerns after approval.
Address the issue directly and professionally with the colleague.
Clarify contributions politely during a team discussion.
Confront the colleague angrily in public.
Fix the error immediately and inform the team.
Delay sending the report until it is reviewed.
Send it anyway and fix it later.
Ignore it because it seems minor
Offer help and ask how you can support them.
Inform the manager without speaking to them.
Wait until they ask for help.
Ask for clarification and begin learning immediately.
Encourage them to resolve the issue privately.
Encourage honesty and help fix the mistake.
Address the behavior politely after the meeting.
Bring it up with the manager.
Suggest a clear improvement with reasoning.
Follow the process without questioning it.
Inform the team immediately and correct it.
Report it immediately and help contain the issue.
Express concerns respectfully with proper reasoning.
Encourage inclusion and invite them to participate.
Mention it privately to the manager.
Negotiate a feasible solution while keeping quality intact.
Accept the request without assessing feasibility.
Complain to colleagues about the client.
Provide structured guidance, feedback, and encouragement.
Let them figure it out on their own.
Do their work for them to save time.
Complain to your manager that they are slow.
Hold a one-on-one conversation to understand their perspective and find common ground.
Ask the manager to override the team member.
Ignore their objections and continue as planned.
Remove the team member from the project.
Privately discuss the issue with them and suggest appropriate use of resources.
Report the behavior to management without confronting them first.
Ignore it because it doesn’t directly affect your work.
Join them in using the resources for personal projects.
A. Watch a video showing how it is played.
